heading: Definitions
label: 53701 Definitions

L. 102–567, title III, § 304106 Stat. 4283Pub. L. 103–160, title XIII, § 1356(1)107 Stat. 1812Pub. L. 104–208, title I, § 101 [title II, § 211(b)]110 Stat. 3009–41Pub. L. 108–136, title XXXV, § 3521(b)117 Stat. 1799July 31, 1959Sept. 2, 1960Sept. 26, 1961Oct. 21, 1970Oct. 19, 1972Aug. 3, 1980Dec. 22, 1980Aug. 6, 1981Nov. 23, 1988Oct. 29, 1992Nov. 30, 1993Sept. 30, 1996Oct. 8, 1996Nov. 24, 200353701(2)46 App.:1271(h).53701(3)46 App.:1271(g).53701(4)46 App.:1271().o53701(5)46 App.:1271(j), (k), (m).53701(6)46 App.:1271().l53701(7)46 App.:1271(a).53701(8)46 App.:1271(c).53701(9)46 App.:1271(e).53701(10)46 App.:1271(d).53701(11)46 App.:1271(i).53701(12)46 App.:1271(n).53701(13)46 App.:1271(b).In paragraph (2), the words “but shall not be limited to” are omitted as unnecessary.In paragraph (4)(B), the words “become or remain” are substituted for “be placed under or continued to be” for clarity.In paragraph (5)(B)(i), the words “individual who is a citizen of the United States” are substituted for “individual who is a citizen or national of the United States or a citizen of the Northern Mariana Islands” in 46 App. U.S.C. 1271(k) because of the definition of “citizen of the United States” in chapter 1 of the revised title. Citizens of the Northern Mariana Islands became citizens or non-citizen nationals of the United States (either of which is a “citizen of the United States” as defined in chapter 1 of the revised title) when the Covenant establishing the Commonwealth of the Northern Mariana Islands became effective on .November 4, 1986In paragraph (5)(B)(ii), the words “corporation, partnership, association, or other” are omitted as suplus [sic]. The words “the term ‘State’ as used therein includes any State, the District of Columbia, the Commonwealth of Puerto Rico, American Samoa, the Virgin Islands of the United States, Guam, the Northern Mariana Islands, or any other Commonwealth, territory, or possession of the United States” in 46 App. U.S.C. 1271(k) are omitted as unnecessary because of the definitions of “State” and “United States” in chapter 1 of the revised title. The definition of “United States” in 46 App. U.S.C. 1271(m) is omitted as unnecessary because of the definition of “United States” in chapter 1. The words “and nationals of the United States or citizens of the Northern Mariana Islands shall be treated as citizens of the United States in meeting such ownership requirement” are omitted as unnecessary because of the definition of “citizen of the United States” in chapter 1 of the revised title.In paragraph (8), before subparagraph (A), the words “instrument of indebtedness” are substituted for “note, bond, debenture, or other evidence of indebtedness” to eliminate unnecessary words. In subparagraph (B), the reference to section 53717 is substituted for the reference to 46 App. U.S.C. 1272 because the accounts under section 53717 replace the Federal Ship Financing Fund. See the explanation for section 53717.

children.label: A
children.text: all amounts paid by or for the account of the obligor as of the date on which a determination is made under ; andsection 53715(d)(1) of this title

children.label: B
children.text: all amounts that the Secretary or Administrator reasonably estimates the obligor will become obligated to pay from time to time thereafter, for the construction, reconstruction, or reconditioning of the vessel, including guarantee fees that will become payable under in connection with all obligations issued for construction, reconstruction, or reconditioning of the vessel or equipment to be delivered, and all obligations issued for the delivered vessel or equipment.section 53714 of this title

children.heading: .—Administrator
children.label: 2 .—Administrator
children.text: The term “Administrator” means the Administrator of the Maritime Administration.

children.heading: .—Construction, reconstruction, and reconditioning
children.label: 3 .—Construction, reconstruction, and reconditioning
children.text: The terms “construction”, “reconstruction”, and “reconditioning” include designing, inspecting, outfitting, and equipping.

children.label: A
children.text: if the vessel was not reconstructed or reconditioned, the actual cost of the vessel depreciated on a straight line basis over the useful life of the vessel as determined by the Secretary or Administrator, not to exceed 25 years from the date of delivery by the builder; or

children.label: i
children.text: the actual cost of the vessel depreciated on a straight line basis from the date of delivery by the builder to the date of the reconstruction or reconditioning, using the original useful life of the vessel, and from the date of the reconstruction or reconditioning, using a useful life of the vessel determined by the Secretary or Administrator; and

children.label: ii
children.text: any amount paid or obligated to be paid for the reconstruction or reconditioning, depreciated on a straight line basis using a useful life of the vessel determined by the Secretary or Administrator.

children.label: I
children.text: a structure or appurtenance thereto designed for the unloading and receiving from vessels, the processing, the holding pending processing, the distribution after processing, or the holding pending distribution, of fish from a fishery;

children.label: II
children.text: the land necessary for the structure or appurtenance; and

children.label: III
children.text: equipment that is for use with the structure or appurtenance and that is necessary for performing a function referred to in subclause (I);

children.label: ii
children.text: for operations not on land, a vessel built in the United States and used for, equipped to be used for, or of a type normally used for, the processing of fish; or

children.label: I
children.text: a structure or appurtenance thereto designed for aquaculture;

children.label: II
children.text: the land necessary for the structure or appurtenance;

children.label: III
children.text: equipment that is for use with the structure or appurtenance and that is necessary for performing a function referred to in subclause (I); and

children.label: IV
children.text: a vessel built in the United States and used for, equipped to be used for, or of a type normally used for, aquaculture.

children.label: i
children.text: an individual who is a citizen of the United States; or

children.label: ii
children.text: an entity that is a citizen of the United States under and that is at least 75 percent owned (as determined under that section) by citizens of the United States.section 50501 of this title

children.heading: .—Fishing vessel
children.label: 6 .—Fishing vessel
children.text: The term “fishing vessel” has the meaning given that term in section 3 of the Magnuson-Stevens Fishery Conservation and Management Act (), and any reference in this chapter to a vessel designed principally for commercial use in the fishing trade or industry is deemed to be a reference to a fishing vessel.16 U.S.C. 1802

children.label: A
children.text: refurbishing, repairing, rebuilding, or replacing equipment on a fishing vessel, without materially increasing harvesting capacity;

children.label: B
children.text: purchasing a used fishing vessel;

children.label: C
children.text: purchasing, constructing, expanding, or reconditioning a fishery facility;

children.label: D
children.text: refinancing existing debt;

children.label: E
children.text: reducing fishing capacity; and

children.label: i
children.text: collection and reporting of fishery-dependent data;

children.label: ii
children.text: bycatch reduction or avoidance;

children.label: iii
children.text: gear selectivity;

children.label: iv
children.text: adverse impacts caused by fishing gear; or

children.label: v
children.text: safety.

children.label: A
children.text: a preferred mortgage as defined in ; andsection 31301 of this title

children.label: B
children.text: a mortgage on a vessel that will become a preferred mortgage when filed or recorded under chapter 313 of this title.

children.label: A
children.text: an obligation issued by the Secretary or Administrator under ; andsection 53723 of this title

children.label: B
children.text: an obligation eligible for investment of funds under section 53715(f) or 53717 of this title.

children.heading: .—Obligee
children.label: 10 .—Obligee
children.text: The term “obligee” means the holder of an obligation.

children.heading: .—Obligor
children.label: 11 .—Obligor
children.text: The term “obligor” means a party primarily liable for payment of the principal of or interest on an obligation.

children.heading: .—Ocean thermal energy conversion facility or plantship
children.label: 12 .—Ocean thermal energy conversion facility or plantship
A
children.text: equipment installed on the facility or vessel to use the electricity or other form of energy to produce, process, refine, or manufacture a product;

children.label: B
children.text: a cable or pipeline used to deliver the electricity, freshwater, or product to shore; and

children.label: C
children.text: other associated equipment and appurtenances of the facility or vessel to the extent they are located seaward of the high water mark.

children.heading: .—Secretary
children.label: 13 .—Secretary
children.text: The term “Secretary” means the Secretary of Commerce with respect to fishing vessels and fishery facilities.

children.label: A
children.text: a cargo vessel;

children.label: B
children.text: a passenger vessel;

children.label: C
children.text: a combination cargo and passenger vessel;

children.label: D
children.text: a tanker;

children.label: E
children.text: a tug or towboat;

children.label: F
children.text: a barge;

children.label: G
children.text: a dredge;

children.label: H
children.text: a floating drydock with a capacity of at least 35,000 lifting tons and a beam of at least 125 feet between the wing walls;

children.label: I
children.text: an oceanographic research vessel;

children.label: J
children.text: an instruction vessel;

children.label: K
children.text: a pollution treatment, abatement, or control vessel;

children.label: L
children.text: a fishing vessel whose ownership meets the citizenship requirements under for documenting vessels to operate in the coastwise trade; andsection 50501 of this title

children.label: M
children.text: an ocean thermal energy conversion facility or plantship that is or will be documented under the laws of the United States.

children.heading: .—Vessel of national interest
children.label: 15 .—Vessel of national interest
children.text: The term “Vessel of National Interest” means a vessel deemed to be of national interest that meets characteristics determined by the Administrator, in consultation with the Secretary of Defense, the Secretary of the Department in which the Coast Guard is operating when it is not operating as a service in the Department of the Navy, or the heads of other Federal agencies, as described in section 53703(d).
